title: [APIv2] pod kill: "signal" parameter doesn't work
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->
With "signal" parameter setting, pod should exit with differnet exitcode. podman cli works as expected, but APIv2 maybe not implement this parameter.

[root@kvm-02-guest22 ~]# podman pod start hugo
4698bcdebe125a9f7b0667696e72bd5bcd6a1bc0d915a2986d289ac79883f46e
[root@kvm-02-guest22 ~]# podman pod kill hugo
4698bcdebe125a9f7b0667696e72bd5bcd6a1bc0d915a2986d289ac79883f46e
[root@kvm-02-guest22 ~]# podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                           COMMAND               CREATED     STATUS                       PORTS  NAMES
73306c001411  docker.io/library/nginx:alpine  nginx -g daemon o...  2 days ago  Exited (137) 19 seconds ago         practical_liskov
92a83033bc0b  k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.2                                  2 days ago  Exited (137) 19 seconds ago         4698bcdebe12-infra
[root@kvm-02-guest22 ~]# podman pod start hugo
4698bcdebe125a9f7b0667696e72bd5bcd6a1bc0d915a2986d289ac79883f46e
[root@kvm-02-guest22 ~]# podman pod kill -s TERM hugo
4698bcdebe125a9f7b0667696e72bd5bcd6a1bc0d915a2986d289ac79883f46e
[root@kvm-02-guest22 ~]# podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                           COMMAND               CREATED     STATUS                    PORTS  NAMES
73306c001411  docker.io/library/nginx:alpine  nginx -g daemon o...  2 days ago  Exited (0) 3 seconds ago         practical_liskov
92a83033bc0b  k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.2                                  2 days ago  Exited (0) 3 seconds ago         4698bcdebe12-infra


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. podman system service --time 0 tcp:127.0.0.1:8081 &> ~/server.log &

2. 
[root@kvm-02-guest22 ~]# podman pod start hugo
4698bcdebe125a9f7b0667696e72bd5bcd6a1bc0d915a2986d289ac79883f46e
[root@kvm-02-guest22 ~]# curl -X POST -H 'Content-type: application/json' -d '{"signal":"TERM"}' http://127.0.0.1:8080/v1.40//libpod/containers/hugo/kill
[root@kvm-02-guest22 ~]# podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                           COMMAND               CREATED     STATUS                 PORTS  NAMES
73306c001411  docker.io/library/nginx:alpine  nginx -g daemon o...  2 days ago  Up About a minute ago         practical_liskov
92a83033bc0b  k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.2                                  2 days ago  Up About a minute ago         4698bcdebe12-infra
[root@kvm-02-guest22 ~]# curl -X POST -H 'Content-type: application/json' -d '{"signal":"TERM"}' http://127.0.0.1:8080/v1.40/libpod/pods/hugo/kill -v
Note: Unnecessary use of -X or --request, POST is already inferred.
*   Trying 127.0.0.1:8080...
* Connected to 127.0.0.1 (127.0.0.1) port 8080 (#0)
> POST /v1.40/libpod/pods/hugo/kill HTTP/1.1
> Host: 127.0.0.1:8080
> User-Agent: curl/7.69.1
> Accept: */*
> Content-type: application/json
> Content-Length: 17
> 
* upload completely sent off: 17 out of 17 bytes
* Mark bundle as not supporting multiuse
< HTTP/1.1 200 OK
< Content-Type: application/json
< Date: Thu, 28 May 2020 19:16:04 GMT
< Content-Length: 86
< 
{"Errs":null,"Id":"4698bcdebe125a9f7b0667696e72bd5bcd6a1bc0d915a2986d289ac79883f46e"}
* Connection #0 to host 127.0.0.1 left intact
[root@kvm-02-guest22 ~]# podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                           COMMAND               CREATED     STATUS                      PORTS  NAMES
73306c001411  docker.io/library/nginx:alpine  nginx -g daemon o...  2 days ago  Exited (137) 9 seconds ago         practical_liskov
92a83033bc0b  k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.2                                  2 days ago  Exited (137) 9 seconds ago         4698bcdebe12-infra
[root@kvm-02-guest22 ~]# podman pod start hugo
4698bcdebe125a9f7b0667696e72bd5bcd6a1bc0d915a2986d289ac79883f46e
[root@kvm-02-guest22 ~]# curl -X POST -H 'Content-type: application/json' -d '{"signal":"SIGKILL"}' http://127.0.0.1:8080/v1.40/libpod/pods/hugo/kill -v
Note: Unnecessary use of -X or --request, POST is already inferred.
*   Trying 127.0.0.1:8080...
* Connected to 127.0.0.1 (127.0.0.1) port 8080 (#0)
> POST /v1.40/libpod/pods/hugo/kill HTTP/1.1
> Host: 127.0.0.1:8080
> User-Agent: curl/7.69.1
> Accept: */*
> Content-type: application/json
> Content-Length: 20
> 
* upload completely sent off: 20 out of 20 bytes
* Mark bundle as not supporting multiuse
< HTTP/1.1 200 OK
< Content-Type: application/json
< Date: Thu, 28 May 2020 19:16:56 GMT
< Content-Length: 86
< 
{"Errs":null,"Id":"4698bcdebe125a9f7b0667696e72bd5bcd6a1bc0d915a2986d289ac79883f46e"}
* Connection #0 to host 127.0.0.1 left intact
[root@kvm-02-guest22 ~]# podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                           COMMAND               CREATED     STATUS                      PORTS  NAMES
73306c001411  docker.io/library/nginx:alpine  nginx -g daemon o...  2 days ago  Exited (137) 6 seconds ago         practical_liskov
92a83033bc0b  k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.2                                  2 days ago  Exited (137) 6 seconds ago         4698bcdebe12-infra


**Describe the results you received:**
curl -X POST -H 'Content-type: application/json' -d '{"signal":"SIGKILL"}' http://127.0.0.1:8080/v1.40/libpod/pods/hugo/kill -v
curl -X POST -H 'Content-type: application/json' -d '{"signal":"TERM"}' http://127.0.0.1:8080/v1.40/libpod/pods/hugo/kill -v
With 2 different signals, but the exitcodes are both 137. 

**Describe the results you expected:**
With signal "SIGKILL", exitcode should be 137, but "TERM" should be 0

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
[root@kvm-02-guest22 ~]# podman version
Version:            1.9.3
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.14.2
Git Commit:         f65ad0dda7ffef9397d1aaa47259ad4d4f12474f
Built:              Mon May 25 13:51:06 2020
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64

```

Built:              Mon May 25 13:51:06 2020
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
[root@kvm-02-guest22 ~]# podman info --debug

  gitCommit: f65ad0dda7ffef9397d1aaa47259ad4d4f12474f

      crun version 0.13
      commit: e79e4de4ac16da0ce48777afb72c6241de870525

      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL

beaker vm with fedora32

comments.body:
-d on curl sends the information within the body of the POST.  For some commands, like container create, it is valid to send the data in the body as json.  However, in this case, the API requires path and query parameters.  So you must send like so:

`curl -X POST .... http:/.../libpod/pods/hug/kill?signal=term`

where hugo is a path parameter representing 'name' and 'signal' is a query parameter.
Yes. it works now. thanks Brent
